description: Master the art of building highly resilient, fault-tolerant, and horizontally scalable distributed systems. This roadmap guides you through the complex structural patterns required to orchestrate high-throughput traffic, decouple services cleanly, handle data consistency at scale, and mitigate systemic failure modes.

topics.title: High-Availability Load Balancing & Traffic Routing
topics.note: Move past simple round-robin setups to understand deep application-layer routing, health checks, and global traffic networks.

subtopics.title: Analyze the mechanical differences between Layer 4 (TCP-level) and Layer 7 (Application-level) load balancing, optimizing routing choices for sticky sessions and SSL termination
resources: System Design Primer: High Availability and Load Balancers
NGINX Architecture: Core Performance Tuning Manual

subtopics.title: Implement advanced routing algorithms (Weighted Round Robin, Least Connections, Consistent Hashing) to prevent cache stampedes and minimize hotspotting
resources: Consistent Hashing Mechanics and Distributed Ring Topologies

subtopics.title: Architect Global Server Load Balancing (GSLB) setups utilizing Anycast routing and latency-based DNS routing across multiple geographic cloud regions
resources: AWS Route 53 & Cloudflare: Designing Resilient Global Traffic Managers

topics.title: Microservices Decomposition & Distributed Isolation
topics.note: Learn to slice complex monolothic logic into robust, maintainable service boundaries while managing inter-service communication overhead.

subtopics.title: Define strict service domains using Domain-Driven Design (DDD) principles, mapping out bounded contexts, aggregate roots, and context maps
resources: Domain-Driven Design: Tackling Complexity in Software (Eric Evans)

subtopics.title: Design highly resilient synchronous service-to-service communication paths using gRPC or internal REST APIs protected by automated Circuit Breakers (e.g., Netflix Hystrix pattern)
resources: Microservice Architecture Patterns: Circuit Breakers and Retries with Exponential Backoff

subtopics.title: Implement the API Gateway pattern as a unified entry point, offloading horizontal cross-cutting concerns like global authentication tracking, rate limiting, and request tracing
resources: Designing Distributed Gateways: Envoy and Kong Architecture Layouts

subtopics.title: Establish end-to-end distributed tracing using OpenTelemetry, attaching unique correlation IDs to incoming requests to map deep execution graphs across boundaries
resources: Distributed Systems Observability: Logging, Metrics, and Tracing Metrics

topics.title: Async Event-Driven Systems & Messaging Architecture
topics.note: Master asynchronous processing patterns to fully decouple storage states, maximize system throughput, and maintain ledger consistency.

subtopics.title: Analyze structural differences between message queues (RabbitMQ/SQS) and distributed log stores (Apache Kafka), picking the right model based on message lifecycle needs
resources: Designing Data-Intensive Applications (Martin Kleppmann): Chapter 11 Stream Processing
Kafka Internals: Partitions, Consumer Groups, and Commit Log Offsets

subtopics.title: Architect multi-service distributed transactions handling business loops safely using the Saga Pattern (Orchestration vs. Choreography structures)
resources: Distributed Transactions At Scale: Managing Concurrency via Sagas

subtopics.title: Implement Event Sourcing together with Command Query Responsibility Segregation (CQRS) patterns to optimize separate write and read paths
resources: CQRS and Event Sourcing Architectural Blueprint Specifications

subtopics.title: Ensure transactional correctness by designing strictly Idempotent message consumers capable of dropping duplicate events out without data corruption
resources: Handling Exactly-Once Semantics and Deduplication in Consumer Services

topics.title: Advanced Scalability, Caching & Data Consistency
topics.note: Address physical performance boundaries by establishing distributed memory tiers and database horizontal scale strategies.

subtopics.title: Design distributed caching clusters using Redis or Memcached, implementing Cache-Aside, Write-Through, and Refresh-Ahead strategies while avoiding cache stampedes
resources: System-Level Caching Patterns and Cache Invalidation Challenges

subtopics.title: Implement database scaling operations via horizontal partitioning (Sharding) and read-replicas, choosing appropriate shard keys to completely eliminate hot shards
resources: Database Sharding Architecture: Rules, Mechanics, and Topologies

subtopics.title: Master the trade-offs of the CAP Theorem and PACELC theorem, balancing network partitions against strong consistency vs. eventual consistency
resources: CAP Theorem vs. PACELC: Choosing Consistency States in Distributed Engines

topics.title: Distributed System Design Blueprint Capstone
topics.note: Synthesize all distributed patterns together into a complete high-concurrency production-grade architecture document.

subtopics.title: Design a complete, production-ready distributed architecture for a high-throughput multi-user platform: draft detailed technical design documentation detailing API gateways, L7 load balancing rings, database horizontal shards, and asymmetric Redis caching arrays

Incorporate an asynchronous event-driven layer inside your architecture: detail how an update operation streams through a Kafka log broker to feed an event-sourced audit log database and sync real-time search vector collections concurrently without bottlenecking core HTTP write paths
resources: Enterprise Architecture Patterns: Designing Resilient Event Infrastructures
